summary: Matched the common forward smash (`ftCo_AttackS4`) path by replacing a large block of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with a new C implementation and header. The PR also cleaned up related fighter input names, exposed helper prototypes, renamed stick-angle helpers, moved an `ABS` macro to shared math code, and adjusted the object file order so `ftCo_AttackS4.c` builds in the correct text position.

key_files.role: new matched C implementation for forward smash input handling, item-throw interaction, character-specific enter routing, animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks
new public prototypes for `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`, `ftCo_AttackS4_8008C114`, and action callbacks
large assembly deletion corresponding to the newly matched AttackS4 code
inserts `ftCo_AttackS4.c.o` before remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` to preserve object/text ordering
renames fighter input fields from `lsubstick`/`lsubstick1` to `cstick`/`cstick1` and renames common-data field `x98_shieldHealthInit` to `x98`
propagates the c-stick field rename through input initialization and input update code
renames angle helpers to `ftCo_GetLStickAngle` and `ftCo_GetCStickAngle`; c-stick helper now reads `fp->input.cstick`
moves the local `ABS` macro out of `ftCo_AttackS3.c` into shared math code for reuse by AttackS4
deleted obsolete jump-table stub now represented by C-side logic in `decideFighter`

what_changed: Added `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`, which checks A-button/l-stick smash input via `checkLStick`, falls back to c-stick detection via `ft_800DF1C8`, computes stick sign and stick angle, handles item throws first, then enters the appropriate forward-smash motion.

Added `ftCo_AttackS4_8008C114`, a variant using facing-relative l-stick input (`checkFacingDir`) before the same c-stick fallback, item-throw check, and fighter-decision path; the source includes a TODO noting it may be combinable with `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`.

Added `checkItemThrow`, preserving item-specific behavior: light throw forward/back when LR is held, item type is 0, item type 3 is blocked by `it_8026B594`, or `ft_800DF21C` is true; item type 2 calls `ftCo_Attack_800CCF58(gobj, 2)` after setting facing direction; item type 3 calls `ftCo_Attack_800CDD14(gobj)`.

Added `decideFighter`, replacing an assembly jump table with a C switch over `fp->kind`: Ness, Peach, and Game & Watch dispatch to character-specific AttackS4 enter functions; Pikachu/Pichu use normal entry plus hitlag pause/resume effect callbacks; all others use the common enter path.

Added `doEnter`, which selects `AttackS4` motion variants from stick-angle thresholds and availability checks through `ftData_80085FD4`, clears interrupt/cmd/throw state, calls `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, then starts animation with `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.

Added standard action callbacks: `ftCo_AttackS4_Anim` exits through `ft_8008A2BC` when no frames remain, `ftCo_AttackS4_IASA` performs interrupt/action checks through `GUARD`, `ftCo_AttackS4_Phys` calls `ft_80084FA8`, and `ftCo_AttackS4_Coll` calls `ft_80084104`.
Renamed helper functions `ftCommon_8007D964` and `ftCommon_8007D99C` to the more descriptive `ftCo_GetLStickAngle` and `ftCo_GetCStickAngle`, and updated AttackS3/Hi3/Lw3 call sites.
Renamed fighter input fields `lsubstick` and `lsubstick1` to `cstick` and `cstick1`, with corresponding changes in fighter input reset/update logic and c-stick angle calculation.

Replaced some anonymous input-check function names with clearer names: `ft_800D695C` became `ftCo_Attack100_CheckInput`, and `ft_800D8990` became `ftCo_Catch_CheckInput`, with usage updates in Wait, Link special, Samus special, and Yoshi guard code.
Deleted `src/melee/ft/ft_3C54.c`, which previously held a jump table of static stubs for AttackS4 routing.
smart_moves: Factored repeated forward-smash input code into small static helpers (`checkLStick`, `checkFacingDir`, `checkItemThrow`, `decideFighter`, `doEnter`) instead of translating the assembly as one large monolithic function.
Preserved matching-sensitive object ordering by inserting `ftCo_AttackS4.c.o` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` rather than simply appending the new object.
Used descriptive stick-angle helper names and c-stick field names to make later input decompilation work easier to search and reason about.
Converted an assembly jump-table pattern into a readable `switch (fp->kind)` while retaining special cases for Ness, Peach, Game & Watch, Pikachu, and Pichu.

Kept uncertain structure/function knowledge explicit: the PR uses names like `cmd_unk0_bool` and leaves a TODO that `ftCo_AttackS4_8008C114` might be combined with `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`, avoiding overclaiming semantics.
Moved the `ABS` macro from AttackS3 into shared `math.h`, allowing AttackS4 to reuse the same expression while documenting uncertainty about whether `fabs_inline` is fake.
decomp_lessons: When matching common fighter action code, expect adjacent input helper names and struct fields to need cleanup; matching AttackS4 required clearer l-stick/c-stick angle helpers and c-stick input field names.
For fighter action input checks, small helper functions can reproduce assembly control flow while making semantic branches clearer, especially for l-stick vs c-stick fallback logic.
Object file placement is part of the match: moving a block from asm to C may require reordering unrelated-looking objects in `obj_files.mk` to keep the same text layout.
Jump tables in asm can often be represented as C `switch` statements over fighter kind when case-specific entry functions are known.
Header exposure should be minimal but sufficient: formerly static/unknown functions used by the new C implementation were promoted in headers only where the diff needed them.
Use address comments in headers for newly matched functions to preserve searchability and continuity with the original assembly addresses.
Keep uncertain names honest. This PR retained address-based names such as `ftCo_AttackS4_8008C114` and `ft_800DF1C8` where semantics were not fully established.

naming_conventions.lesson: Prefer semantic helper names once usage shows the function computes the l-stick angle.
Rename paired helpers together to clarify l-stick/c-stick symmetry.
Use gameplay/control terminology when a substick field is clearly the C-stick.
Keep historical/current input-buffer field naming parallel after renaming the base field.
Anonymous action-check functions can be renamed as their role in IASA/input chains becomes clear.
Use `ftCo_*_CheckInput` naming for common action input predicates.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed approximately 696 lines of `ft_08A4.s` while adding a 227-line C file for the matched AttackS4 implementation.
Used `ABS(x) ((x) < 0 ? -(x) : (x))` instead of relying solely on `fabs_inline`, with a TODO noting matching uncertainty.
Matched stick-sign selection with ternary expressions returning explicit `+1.0f` or `-1.0f` values.
Matched item-throw branching by preserving repeated calls to `it_8026B30C` and separate item type cases rather than aggressively caching or refactoring.
Replaced an asm jump table (`jtbl_803C54D8` and labels such as `ft_8008C388`, `ft_8008C390`, `ft_8008C398`) with a C switch in `decideFighter`.
Kept remaining unknown functions as address-style declarations in headers (`ft_800DF1C8`, `ft_800DF21C`, `ftCo_800CECE8`) to unblock matching without inventing names.

review_feedback.note: The PR context contains no issue comments, review comments, or review bodies; no reviewer feedback can be inferred from the available evidence.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftCo_AttackS4_8008C114` or merge it with `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput`.
Find later PRs that resolve the TODO about `ABS` versus `fabs_inline` in stick-angle checks.
Search for follow-up naming of `ft_800DF1C8`, `ft_800DF21C`, `ftCo_800CECE8`, and `ftCo_800CED30`.
Check whether later decompilation reuses the `cstick`/`cstick1` field names across all fighter input code.
Compare this AttackS4 implementation with later matched AttackHi4 and AttackLw4 code for shared input and item-throw patterns.
